Off Calendar Feb-05-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susan McElhinney appeared by telephone for Joan Decker.
Paul Tour-Sarkissian and Phil Foster for Janet Molinari.
Nancy Field for and with Ann Smith (Trustee).

Ms. McElhinney requested that the matter go off calendar. Ms. Field stated that she has no problem with the matter going off calendar in light of the conflict between the Beneficiaries and the conflict between the Beneficiaries and the Trustee, that her Client ran into significant problems (more damage found) when getting the repairs done, that she can do a petition or the Court can authorize use of additional funds, and that the Beneficiaries can think about appointment of another Trustee. The Court informed Ms. Field that another petition needs to be filed. Mr. Foster stated that he agrees another petition should be filed, and that he feels the parties should meet and confer as to a new professional fiduciary and consider stipulating to an order to allow the additional repairs to be done. Ms. Field stated that she feels her Client should finish up what she was ordered to do so that the new fiduciary can start clean. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. is off calendar. The Court reminds Counsel that what ever needs to be done will require filing of another petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Field for and with (trustee) Anne F. Smith. Susan McElhinney (via court call) for (beneficiary) Joan Decker.

The Court stated that this is a petition for fees and costs, that an objection has been made by Ms. Decker and asked counsel for oral argument regarding settlement. Ms. McElhinney stated that in regards to the request for instructions her client is unclear as to how to proceed on the sale of the Oregon property, that they do not know the current status of money in the trust, and that if the money in the trust is for the Cabrillo property if it will have an effect on the Oregon property. The Court stated that counsel complied with the local rule regarding providing an accounting for 2 years. Ms. Field stated that as of today there is $106,100.69 in the trust, that there are fees to be paid, that Ms. Decker is ignoring that the Court ordered the Long Beach and Oregon properties to be sold, that everyone has been noticed on everything filed, that Ms. Decker and the other beneficiaries have had counsel throughout the proceedings, that they are concerned as to why Ms. Decker never objected before but is coming in now and that she does not cite case-law in any of her papers. The Court stated that an accounting should be made for the 3 year period. Ms. McElhinney stated that this is a late accounting, that they received a letter from the trustee stating that the accounting would be provided in August of 2015 but has just now been filed, that the communication issues date back to 2010, that the request for attorney's fees is for services provided 4 years ago, that there are a lot of private attorney fees which should have been covered by insurance, that they did not file a formal objection to the sale of the Long Beach property but did send letters objecting, that the Oregon property has not been sold because the beneficiaries do not want it sold, that this should have been dealt with in 2010/2011 and that the Court has the power to adjust the compensation. Ms. Field agreed that the accounting is late, that they have communicated with Ms. Decker and her son throughout the proceedings, that they retained Mr. Hill and it was he who got the insurance company involved, that his fees are only $21,000.00 and he did a very good job on behalf of the estate and that the trustee's fees are reduced per the hourly rate. Ms. McElhinney stated that they agree with the request to reduce the reserve on Cabrillo and the dispensing of notice. The Court stated that the beneficiaries are interested in purchasing the Oregon property and that the order is to include that the 3 beneficiaries have the first right to purchase the property. Ms. Fields stated that they will want to have a formal appraisal of the Oregon property and that the beneficiaries will have 30 days from the mailing of the appraisal to respond with a yes or no as to the purchase of the property. The Court stated that it is ordering that an accounting for the period of 2013-2016 be filed by February 1, 2016. Ms. Field is to prepare a modified order. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
